conditions.today: It's Sunday Fun Day! Come and celebrate the sunshine as Rendezvous Mountain will be engulfed in Bluebird skies all day long. Morning ski conditions will be fast and fantastic while they soften up throughout the day. On the Mountain, look for calm ridgetop winds trending out of the Southwest at 5-10 MPH. Afternoon temperatures will reach the mid-20's across the high elevation terrain while the valley floor will climb into the upper 30's by late morning. Set your cruise control to mellow and come massage the slopes of The Big One with the edges of your planks. It will be a good day to ski any and all terrain. Stay safe out there! -MB
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM—Greetings and welcome to the weekend, Jackson. I suggest you don't sleep in on what could be a sneaky little Saturday morning, thanks to a two-inch overnight adorning. Early clouds and stray snow showers will fade away, leaving partly cloudy conditions for the afternoon. Warmer, but still cool, high temperatures will just reach the double digits outside Corbet's Cabin and right around the 30-degree mark in the Village. West winds in the 5-20 mph range will keep the photons refracting from the vapor clouds lingering in your wake. Come out and let your common consciousness undergo a crystal alteration. But beware, a subtle insanity comes in the aftermath of this mystical revelation. -Lege

trends: Great week for snow - 26" in last 7 days
recommendations: Fresh corduroy on Tram Line - perfect for intermediate skiers
Fresh corduroy on St. John's - perfect for intermediate skiers
Fresh corduroy on Ranger - challenging for advanced skiers

morningBrief.headline: Good Snow Persisting
morningBrief.body: Zero new inches, but who's complaining? 26" this week means conditions are still prime.
